input.description: Find fzf and border config in dotfiles
input.prompt:
[CONTEXT]: User wants borderless FZF behavior in fish-integrated FZF flows, matching existing borderless TMUX/plugin style in this dotfiles repo.
[GOAL]: Identify all fish-side FZF integration points and any existing borderless style settings (especially in tmux/plugins) we should mirror.
[DOWNSTREAM]: I will modify only necessary config paths consistently.
[REQUEST]: Return relevant file paths, key variables/options, and where border/border-label or window options are defined. Skip unrelated themes.

todos.content: Locate fish and tmux fzf configuration points and existing borderless settings patterns
Draft exact minimal plan to apply borderless fzf style across fish-integrated fzf usages
Submit implementation plan for user approval via submit_plan

input.summary: Found three fish-side fzf paths; proposing minimal borderless alignment before code changes.
input.plan:
Interpretation: apply borderless `fzf` behavior across fish-integrated flows only (not broader theme changes), matching your existing tmux borderless style.

Acceptance criteria (binary):
1) All fish `fzf` entry points we own include `--border=none` (or equivalent in their default opts).
2) Existing behavior (bindings, preview commands, keybinds) remains unchanged.
3) Fish config remains syntactically valid and diagnostics-clean.
4) Manual QA shows launched fish `fzf` pickers render without outer border.

Planned changes:
1) Update fallback defaults in `fish/functions/_fzf_wrapper.fish` so fallback `FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS` uses borderless mode.
2) Update `fish/conf.d/50-fifc.fish` default `FIFC_FZF_OPTS` to include `--border=none`.
3) Update `fish/functions/_fifc.fish` hardcoded `fzf` invocation to include `--border=none`.

Verification plan:
- Run `lsp_diagnostics` on modified fish files.
- Run a fish syntax check via `fish -n` for modified files.
- Manual QA by invoking a representative picker (`fifc`/`_fifc` path and standard `_fzf_wrapper` path) and confirming no outer frame.
- Report exact commands and outcomes.

input.summary: Revised after deep tmux-style review: fish changes will mirror your exact tmux pattern (`--border=none` and keep preview separator only).
input.plan:
What I verified first (per your feedback):
- `tmux/sesh-fzf-picker.sh` uses `--border=none` and keeps only preview separator via `--preview-window='...:border-left'`.
- `.tmux.conf` enforces the same idea for fuzzback: `--border=none --preview-window=right:60%:nowrap:border-left`.
So your tmux standard is clear: no outer frame; if preview exists, keep only left divider.

Interpretation for fish: apply exactly this border policy to all fish-integrated `fzf` entry points in your config (no theme or behavior expansion).

Acceptance criteria (pass/fail):
1) Every fish-owned `fzf` default path has outer border disabled (`--border=none`).
2) Existing preview layouts remain the same except preserving divider behavior where preview already exists.
3) No keybind, source, or selection logic changes.
4) Fish files pass syntax check and diagnostics; manual run confirms borderless UI.

Planned edits (minimal):
1) `fish/functions/_fzf_wrapper.fish`
   - Change fallback default from `--border` to `--border=none` (for any path where `FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS` is not already exported).
2) `fish/conf.d/50-fifc.fish`
   - Add `--border=none` to default `FIFC_FZF_OPTS`.
3) `fish/functions/_fifc.fish`
   - Add `--border=none` to the hardcoded `fzf` invocation so custom completion path follows same style.

Verification plan:
- `lsp_diagnostics` on modified fish files.
- `fish -n` on modified fish files.
- Manual QA:
  - Run a standard `_fzf_wrapper` picker path (directory/history search).
  - Run a `fifc`/`_fifc` path.
  - Confirm no outer border in both.
- Report commands and observed results.

input.prompt:
<system-reminder>

[SYSTEM DIRECTIVE: OH-MY-OPENCODE - SINGLE TASK ONLY]

**STOP. READ THIS BEFORE PROCEEDING.**

If you were NOT given **exactly ONE atomic task**, you MUST:

**WARNING TO ORCHESTRATOR:**
- Your hasty batching RUINS deliverables
- Each task needs FULL attention and PROPER verification  
- Batch delegation = sloppy work = rework = wasted tokens

**REFUSE multi-task requests. DEMAND single-task clarity.**
</system-reminder>

<Work_Context>
## Notepad Location (for recording learnings)
NOTEPAD PATH: .sisyphus/notepads/{plan-name}/
- learnings.md: Record patterns, conventions, successful approaches
- issues.md: Record problems, blockers, gotchas encountered
- decisions.md: Record architectural choices and rationales
- problems.md: Record unresolved issues, technical debt

IMPORTANT: Always APPEND to notepad files - never overwrite or use Edit tool.

## Plan Location (READ ONLY)
PLAN PATH: .sisyphus/plans/{plan-name}.md

CRITICAL RULE: NEVER MODIFY THE PLAN FILE

The plan file (.sisyphus/plans/*.md) is SACRED and READ-ONLY.
- You may READ the plan to understand tasks
- You may READ checkbox items to know what to do
- You MUST NOT edit, modify, or update the plan file
- You MUST NOT mark checkboxes as complete in the plan
- Only the Orchestrator manages the plan file

VIOLATION = IMMEDIATE FAILURE. The Orchestrator tracks plan state.
</Work_Context>
## 1. TASK
Implement borderless fzf everywhere in fish-integrated fzf flows, mirroring the existing tmux pattern (no outer frame; keep preview divider when preview is present).

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME
- [ ] Files modified: `fish/functions/_fzf_wrapper.fish`
- [ ] Files modified: `fish/conf.d/40-plugins.fish` (ONLY if needed; prefer not)
- [ ] Files modified: `fish/conf.d/50-fifc.fish`
- [ ] Files modified: `fish/functions/_fifc.fish`
- [ ] Functionality: fish-owned fzf entry points default to `--border=none`
- [ ] Functionality: preview window dividers (e.g. `border-left`) remain intact where previews exist
- [ ] Verification: `fish -n` succeeds for all modified files

## 3. REQUIRED TOOLS
- read: inspect current opts in fish fzf files before editing
- grep: find any other fish-owned fzf invocations that hardcode border
- apply_patch: make minimal edits (no formatting churn)
- bash: run `fish -n <file>` for each modified file

## 4. MUST DO
- Mirror tmux approach exactly: use `--border=none` (see `.tmux.conf` and `tmux/sesh-fzf-picker.sh` patterns)
- Keep existing behavior: no changes to keybindings, prompts, colors, preview commands, sorting
- If a file already exports `FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS` (it does), do NOT fight it; ensure borderless is consistent across all entry points we own
- Append a short note to `.sisyphus/notepads/borderless-fzf-fish/decisions.md` describing what was changed and why

## 5. MUST NOT DO
- Do NOT add dependencies
- Do NOT change tmux files
- Do NOT change unrelated fish config
- Do NOT commit, push, or run git commands

## 6. CONTEXT
### Notepad Paths
- READ: `.sisyphus/notepads/borderless-fzf-fish/learnings.md`
- READ: `.sisyphus/notepads/borderless-fzf-fish/issues.md`
- WRITE: append to `.sisyphus/notepads/borderless-fzf-fish/decisions.md`

### Inherited Wisdom
- tmux standard in this repo: disable outer border via `--border=none`; when preview exists, keep separator via `--preview-window=...:border-left`.

### Dependencies
- Fish fzf integration points found:
  - `fish/conf.d/40-plugins.fish` exports `FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS` and already sets `--border=none`.
  - `fish/conf.d/50-fifc.fish` sets `FIFC_FZF_OPTS` used by `fzf` call.
  - `fish/functions/_fifc.fish` builds a hardcoded `fzf` command string.
  - `fish/functions/_fzf_wrapper.fish` has a fallback `FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS` with `--border` (should be made borderless for consistency when used standalone).

input.description: Check zoxide query interactive options and env variables

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: fish/conf.d/40-plugins.fish
@@
 # fzf options (colors/theme)
 set -gx fzf_fd_opts --hidden --exclude=.git
 set -gx fzf_preview_file_cmd 'bat --style=plain --color=always --theme Nord'
 set -gx fzf_directory_opts --color=border:#81A1C1
 set -gx fzf_git_log_opts --color=border:#81A1C1
 set -gx fzf_git_status_opts --color=border:#81A1C1
 set -gx fzf_history_opts --color=border:#81A1C1
 set -gx fzf_processes_opts --color=border:#81A1C1
 set -gx fzf_variables_opts --color=border:#81A1C1
+
+# zoxide uses `zoxide query --interactive` which may pass its own `--border`.
+# Ensure its fzf invocation stays borderless (tmux-style).
+if not set -q _ZO_FZF_OPTS
+    set -gx _ZO_FZF_OPTS "--border=none"
+else if not string match -q -- '*--border=none*' $_ZO_FZF_OPTS
+    set -gx _ZO_FZF_OPTS "$_ZO_FZF_OPTS --border=none"
+end
 set -gx FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS \
     --height=85% \
     --layout=reverse \
     --border=none \
*** End Patch

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: fish/conf.d/40-plugins.fish
@@
-# zoxide uses `zoxide query --interactive` which may pass its own `--border`.
-# Ensure its fzf invocation stays borderless (tmux-style).
-if not set -q _ZO_FZF_OPTS
-    set -gx _ZO_FZF_OPTS "--border=none"
-else if not string match -q -- '*--border=none*' $_ZO_FZF_OPTS
-    set -gx _ZO_FZF_OPTS "$_ZO_FZF_OPTS --border=none"
-end
+# zoxide `query --interactive` swaps its default fzf UI when `_ZO_FZF_OPTS` is set.
+# To change ONLY borders (and keep the exact zoxide UX), set zoxide's default
+# UI flags explicitly but with `--border=none`.
+if not set -q _ZO_FZF_OPTS
+    set -gx _ZO_FZF_OPTS \
+        --exact \
+        --no-sort \
+        --bind=ctrl-z:ignore,btab:up,tab:down \
+        --cycle \
+        --keep-right \
+        --border=none \
+        --height=45% \
+        --info=inline \
+        --layout=reverse \
+        --tabstop=1 \
+        --exit-0 \
+        '--preview=\command -p ls -Cp {2..}' \
+        --preview-window=down,30%,border-top
+end
*** End Patch

input.tmux_command: send-keys -t zocap "env -u _ZO_FZF_OPTS zoxide query --interactive -- dotfiles" Enter
send-keys -t zocap3 "env -u _ZO_FZF_OPTS zoxide query --interactive -- dotfiles" Enter

input.command: set -euo pipefail; ps -ww -o pid=,ppid=,command= -p 12595,75156 2>/dev/null || true

input.command: set -euo pipefail; pgrep -P 75156 -af fzf || true; echo '---'; ps -ww -o pid=,ppid=,command= -p $(pgrep -P 75156 || true) 2>/dev/null || true

input.command: set -euo pipefail; sleep 1; pgrep -af "zoxide query --interactive" || true; echo '---'; ZPID=$(pgrep -f "zoxide query --interactive --" | head -n1 || true); echo ZPID=$ZPID; if [ -n "$ZPID" ]; then pgrep -P "$ZPID" -af fzf || true; ps -ww -o pid=,ppid=,command= -p $(pgrep -P "$ZPID" || true) 2>/dev/null || true; fi

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: fish/conf.d/40-plugins.fish
@@
-# zoxide `query --interactive` swaps its default fzf UI when `_ZO_FZF_OPTS` is set.
-# To change ONLY borders (and keep the exact zoxide UX), set zoxide's default
-# UI flags explicitly but with `--border=none`.
-if not set -q _ZO_FZF_OPTS
-    set -gx _ZO_FZF_OPTS \
-        --exact \
-        --no-sort \
-        --bind=ctrl-z:ignore,btab:up,tab:down \
-        --cycle \
-        --keep-right \
-        --border=none \
-        --height=45% \
-        --info=inline \
-        --layout=reverse \
-        --tabstop=1 \
-        --exit-0 \
-        '--preview=\command -p ls -Cp {2..}' \
-        --preview-window=down,30%,border-top
-end
+# zoxide uses `_ZO_FZF_OPTS` as a full replacement for its built-in fzf UI flags.
+# If it's unset, fzf defaults include an outer border. If it was previously set
+# to a minimal value (e.g. just `--border=none`), zoxide loses its preview and
+# other UX defaults. So we (re-)set the full default flags, changing ONLY the
+# outer border to `--border=none`.
+set -l __zo_reset 0
+if not set -q _ZO_FZF_OPTS
+    set __zo_reset 1
+else if test (count $_ZO_FZF_OPTS) -eq 1; and test "$_ZO_FZF_OPTS[1]" = "--border=none"
+    set __zo_reset 1
+end
+
+if test $__zo_reset -eq 1
+    set -gx _ZO_FZF_OPTS \
+        --exact \
+        --no-sort \
+        --bind=ctrl-z:ignore,btab:up,tab:down \
+        --cycle \
+        --keep-right \
+        --border=none \
+        --height=45% \
+        --info=inline \
+        --layout=reverse \
+        --tabstop=1 \
+        --exit-0 \
+        '--preview=\command -p ls -Cp {2..}' \
+        --preview-window=down,30%,sharp
+end
+
+set -e __zo_reset
 set -gx FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS \
     --height=85% \
     --layout=reverse \
     --border=none \
*** End Patch

input.description: Reproduce zoxide fzf argv with/without _ZO_FZF_OPTS via script pty
input.command:
set -euo pipefail;
run_case() {
  local label="$1"; shift
  echo "== $label ==";
  script -q /dev/null "$@" &
  local spid=$!
  sleep 0.8
  local zpid
  zpid=$(pgrep -P "$spid" -f "zoxide query --interactive" | head -n1 || true)
  echo "script_pid=$spid zoxide_pid=${zpid:-none}";
  if [ -n "${zpid:-}" ]; then
    local fpid
    fpid=$(pgrep -P "$zpid" -f "^fzf" | head -n1 || true)
    echo "fzf_pid=${fpid:-none}";
    [ -n "${fpid:-}" ] && ps -ww -o pid=,ppid=,command= -p "$fpid" || true
  else
    pgrep -af "zoxide query --interactive" || true
    pgrep -af "^fzf" || true
  fi
  # terminate script + children
  kill "$spid" 2>/dev/null || true
  sleep 0.2
}

# Case 1: zoxide defaults
run_case "zoxide default" env -u _ZO_FZF_OPTS zoxide query --interactive -- dotfiles

# Case 2: our intended _ZO_FZF_OPTS (full default, only border change)
run_case "zoxide with _ZO_FZF_OPTS" env _ZO_FZF_OPTS='--exact --no-sort --bind=ctrl-z:ignore,btab:up,tab:down --cycle --keep-right --border=none --height=45% --info=inline --layout=reverse --tabstop=1 --exit-0 --preview=\\command -p ls -Cp {2..} --preview-window=down,30%,sharp' zoxide query --interactive -- dotfiles

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: fish/conf.d/40-plugins.fish
@@
-# zoxide uses `_ZO_FZF_OPTS` as a full replacement for its built-in fzf UI flags.
-# If it's unset, fzf defaults include an outer border. If it was previously set
-# to a minimal value (e.g. just `--border=none`), zoxide loses its preview and
-# other UX defaults. So we (re-)set the full default flags, changing ONLY the
-# outer border to `--border=none`.
-set -l __zo_reset 0
-if not set -q _ZO_FZF_OPTS
-    set __zo_reset 1
-else if test (count $_ZO_FZF_OPTS) -eq 1; and test "$_ZO_FZF_OPTS[1]" = "--border=none"
-    set __zo_reset 1
-end
-
-if test $__zo_reset -eq 1
-    set -gx _ZO_FZF_OPTS \
-        --exact \
-        --no-sort \
-        --bind=ctrl-z:ignore,btab:up,tab:down \
-        --cycle \
-        --keep-right \
-        --border=none \
-        --height=45% \
-        --info=inline \
-        --layout=reverse \
-        --tabstop=1 \
-        --exit-0 \
-        '--preview=\command -p ls -Cp {2..}' \
-        --preview-window=down,30%,sharp
-end
-
-set -e __zo_reset
+# zoxide treats `_ZO_FZF_OPTS` as a full replacement for its built-in fzf UI flags.
+# If it's unset, zoxide uses its built-in defaults (incl. borders). If it was
+# previously set to a minimal value (e.g. just `--border=none`), zoxide loses its
+# preview and other defaults. We therefore (re-)set the full default flags,
+# changing ONLY the outer border to `--border=none`.
+#
+# Important: `_ZO_FZF_OPTS` is parsed as a shell-words string; keep the preview
+# command quoted inside the string.
+if not set -q _ZO_FZF_OPTS; or test "$_ZO_FZF_OPTS" = "--border=none"
+    set -gx _ZO_FZF_OPTS "--exact --no-sort --bind=ctrl-z:ignore,btab:up,tab:down --cycle --keep-right --border=none --height=45% --info=inline --layout=reverse --tabstop=1 --exit-0 --preview='\\command -p ls -Cp {2..}' --preview-window=down,30%,sharp"
+end
*** End Patch

input.description: Verify zoxide sets FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS (print only relevant var)

set -euo pipefail;
script -q /dev/null env _ZO_FZF_OPTS="--exact --no-sort --bind=ctrl-z:ignore,btab:up,tab:down --cycle --keep-right --border=none --height=45% --info=inline --layout=reverse --tabstop=1 --exit-0 --preview='\\command -p ls -Cp {2..}' --preview-window=down,30%,sharp" zoxide query --interactive -- dotfiles &

python - <<'PY'
import subprocess, sys
pid = sys.argv[1]
out = subprocess.check_output(['ps','eww','-p',pid,'-o','command='], text=True, errors='replace')
# Extract only FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS=... token from env dump

else:

PY
"$fpid"

set -euo pipefail;
script -q /dev/null env _ZO_FZF_OPTS="--exact --no-sort --bind=ctrl-z:ignore,btab:up,tab:down --cycle --keep-right --border=none --height=45% --info=inline --layout=reverse --tabstop=1 --exit-0 --preview='\\command -p ls -Cp {2..}' --preview-window=down,30%,sharp" zoxide query --interactive -- dotfiles &

import subprocess, sys

input.description: Extract full FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS from fzf env safely
input.command:
set -euo pipefail;
script -q /dev/null env _ZO_FZF_OPTS="--exact --no-sort --bind=ctrl-z:ignore,btab:up,tab:down --cycle --keep-right --border=none --height=45% --info=inline --layout=reverse --tabstop=1 --exit-0 --preview='\\command -p ls -Cp {2..}' --preview-window=down,30%,sharp" zoxide query --interactive -- dotfiles &
spid=$!
sleep 0.8
zpid=$(pgrep -P "$spid" -f "zoxide query --interactive" | head -n1 || true)
fpid=$(pgrep -P "$zpid" -f "^fzf" | head -n1 || true)
python - "$fpid" <<'PY'
import subprocess, sys, re
pid = sys.argv[1]
out = subprocess.check_output(['ps','eww','-p',pid,'-o','command='], text=True, errors='replace')
# Extract FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS value including spaces, stopping before next ENVVAR=
m = re.search(r'FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS=(.*?)(?= [A-Z0-9_]+=)', out)
print('FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS=' + (m.group(1) if m else 'NOT_FOUND'))
PY
kill "$spid" 2>/dev/null || true

input.summary: Scoped plan for `f` only: patch zoxide’s interactive fzf defaults, changing only `--border=sharp` to `--border=none`.
input.plan:
What I verified:
- `f` in `fish/functions/f.fish` only calls `zi`; the real interactive picker comes from `zoxide init fish` loaded in `fish/conf.d/40-plugins.fish`.
- zoxide 0.9.x hardcodes its interactive `fzf` defaults unless `_ZO_FZF_OPTS` is set.
- If `_ZO_FZF_OPTS` is set, zoxide stops using its built-in defaults, so to change only the border we must restate the full default zoxide fzf option string.
- The current built-in zoxide argv on this machine is:
  - `--exact`
  - `--no-sort`
  - `--bind=ctrl-z:ignore,btab:up,tab:down`
  - `--cycle`
  - `--keep-right`
  - `--border=sharp`
  - `--height=45%`
  - `--info=inline`
  - `--layout=reverse`
  - `--tabstop=1`
  - `--exit-0`
  - preview command `\command -p ls -Cp {2..}`
  - preview window `down,30%,sharp`

Interpretation:
- Change only `f`/`zi`/zoxide interactive UI.
- Change only the outer border.
- Do not change preview presence, preview placement, preview border style, binds, sorting, info display, or general fish `fzf` behavior.

Acceptance criteria (pass/fail):
1) `f` still uses zoxide interactive mode with the same preview, same preview placement, same binds, same list ordering.
2) Outer frame for the zoxide picker is removed (`--border=none`).
3) No non-zoxide fish `fzf` path is changed.
4) `fish -n fish/conf.d/40-plugins.fish` passes.
5) Manual QA confirms the resulting zoxide-spawned `fzf` argv is identical to zoxide defaults except `--border=none` replaces `--border=sharp`.

Planned edit:
1) Update only `fish/conf.d/40-plugins.fish`.
2) In the zoxide init section, set `_ZO_FZF_OPTS` to the full zoxide default options string, but replace `--border=sharp` with `--border=none`.
3) Leave `fish/functions/f.fish` unchanged.
4) Leave `FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS`, `fifc`, `_fzf_wrapper`, and all other fish `fzf` integrations untouched.

Verification plan:
- Run `fish -n fish/conf.d/40-plugins.fish`.
- Manual QA by spawning `zoxide query --interactive` in a pseudo-terminal and capturing the child `fzf` command line.
- Verify the command line still includes all zoxide defaults and preview flags, with only `--border=none` changed from the original `--border=sharp`.
- User-visible check: after `exec fish`, run `f` and confirm only the outer border changed.

## 1. TASK
Remove all scope-creep changes introduced in the last edit, keeping ONLY the intended zoxide border change in `fish/conf.d/40-plugins.fish`.

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME
- [ ] `fish/config.fish` restored to its previous content (undo quote removal and whitespace churn).
